commits.subject: Day 108 (12:54): Fix state failures --recent file-not-found when events.jsonl exists (Task 1)

eval.reason: The implementation correctly replaces strict `read_events()` with lenient `read_tail_events(&path, 0)` in both `handle_failures` and `handle_cache`, adds a new `read_events_lenient()` helper that skips malformed JSON lines via `filter_map`, updates `read_tail_events` limit=0 branch to use it, and adds empty-events guard ("no parseable events found"). Smoke test confirms `state failures --recent --limit 5` now produces actual failure reports from the 7,348-line events.jsonl instead of the old "no state log found" error. `state cache --recent`, `state why`, `state tail`, and `state evals` all continue to work.

task_file_title: Fix state failures --recent file-not-found when events.jsonl exists
